IT PORTFOLIO — COMBINED PROJECT STATUS UPDATE
Reporting Period: March 2026 (Week 1)
Portfolio Manager: Bob Morrison
Distribution: IT Executive Committee; ADM Digital Services

---

PROJECT 1: NGIS PHASE 3 — DATA CENTRE CONSOLIDATION
Project ID: NGIS-P3-2024-001 | Project Manager: Alice Tran
Status: YELLOW | Budget: $2.4M | Deadline: Q2 2026

Summary: Project has encountered a 2-week vendor delay on critical
network equipment. TechSupplier has confirmed revised delivery of March 20
(was March 6). This puts the planned March 18 migration window at risk.
Additionally, a TBS audit finding (ITSG-33 IA-5) was raised on March 5
and must be resolved before migration can proceed.

Key actions in progress:
- Impact assessment on migration timeline (Carol Nguyen, due Mar 7)
- SA&A documentation update for TBS (Dave Chen, due Mar 12)
- Alternative supplier evaluation initiated

Dependencies: NGIS Phase 3 completion is a prerequisite for decommissioning
3 legacy server rooms (Sites A, B, C), which in turn is required before
the GC-Cloud Wave 4 workloads can be migrated.

---

PROJECT 2: GC-CLOUD WAVE 4 — WORKLOAD MIGRATION
Project ID: GCCLOUD-W4-2026 | Project Manager: Carol Nguyen
Status: YELLOW | Budget: $2.4M (revision pending) | Deadline: Q3 2026

Summary: A budget revision request of $340,000 has been submitted to
cover unplanned middleware remediation costs identified during the
infrastructure assessment. The revision requires Director approval by
March 10 and Treasury Board submission by March 15. Project is on pause
for new commitments pending budget approval.

Key actions in progress:
- Director approval of revised budget (deadline: Mar 10)
- TB submission preparation (deadline: Mar 15)
- Middleware remediation scoping (to begin post-approval)

Dependencies: NGIS Phase 3 completion (AD infrastructure must be
migrated before GC-Cloud workloads can be onboarded).

---

PROJECT 3: ATTACK SURFACE MANAGEMENT (ASM) — GATE 2
Project ID: PRJ-1234 | Project Manager: Farida Osei
Status: GREEN | Budget: $1.1M | Deadline: Q4 2026

Summary: Gate 2 review (Business Case and Project Charter) was
successfully completed on February 28, 2026. Steering committee approved
advancement to Gate 3 with no conditions. The project team is now
preparing the procurement strategy for the ASM tooling platform.

Accomplishments:
- Business case approved by ADM
- Project charter signed
- Procurement strategy kick-off scheduled for March 9

No risks or issues at this time. Project is tracking on schedule and
within budget.

---

PORTFOLIO-LEVEL RISKS

1. NGIS-to-GC-Cloud dependency chain: Delays in NGIS Phase 3 will
   cascade to GC-Cloud Wave 4, potentially pushing the Q3 target.
   Portfolio risk level: HIGH.

2. Shared resource constraint: Bob Morrison is the lead technical
   resource on both NGIS Phase 3 (AD migration) and GC-Cloud Wave 4
   (infrastructure architecture). His potential departure (flagged in
   NGIS Sprint Review) would impact both projects simultaneously.
   Risk level: HIGH.

---

Report prepared by: Bob Morrison, Portfolio Manager
